chunk_summary: This section discusses safeguards against micronutrient duplication and the timing of iron supplementation in the management of severe acute malnutrition (SAM), referencing WHO and national guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of avoiding micronutrient duplication in SAM treatment protocols.
Learn the recommended timing and dosage of iron supplementation in SAM care.
Familiarize with WHO and national guidelines for micronutrient management in malnourished children.
key_facts: Micronutrient duplication can lead to toxicity and adverse effects in SAM management.
Iron supplementation should be delayed until the stabilization phase in SAM to avoid complications such as infections.
WHO guidelines emphasize the use of ready-to-use therapeutic foods (RUTF) containing pre-mixed micronutrients to prevent duplication.
The Government of India recommends vitamin A supplementation as part of SAM care protocols.
WHO guidelines highlight the importance of monitoring for nutritional oedema during SAM treatment.
reference_quote: Iron supplementation should be delayed until the stabilization phase in SAM to avoid exacerbating infections. (Source: WHO pocket book of hospital care for children — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154454/)
document_key_topics: WHO and national guidelines for SAM care
